item.title: Bitcoin DVOL Volatility Forecasting with LSTM
item.description: Abstract This master’s thesis presents a comprehensive 17-model benchmark framework for Bitcoin implied volatility (DVOL) forecasting. The research combines traditional financial econometrics with modern deep learning, developing LSTM neural networks that achieve 92.87% R² (MAPE 6.33%, RMSE 5.05, 62% directional accuracy) on out-of-sample test data. A key discovery documents a 13.3% R² degradation artifact in PyTorch DataParallel multi-GPU evaluation, the first such documentation in financial machine learning literature. Key Results LSTM Model Performance (Test Set) Metric Value Interpretation R² Score 0.

item.title: weather_poly: Comprehensive Volatility Forecasting Benchmark
item.description: Project Overview weather_poly is an open-source research repository implementing a comprehensive 17-model benchmark framework for Bitcoin implied volatility (DVOL) forecasting. The project provides a unified pipeline for training, evaluating, and comparing linear models (HAR-RV, OLS), tree-based methods (Random Forest, XGBoost), and deep learning approaches (LSTM). Key Contributions 1. Unified Benchmark Framework 17 models trained and evaluated on identical preprocessing pipeline 5 Linear Models: HAR-RV (canonical), OLS variants with different feature sets 8 Tree Models: Random Forest and XGBoost with lag/jump configurations 6 LSTM Models: Market lags, jump-aware, and hybrid configurations 2.
